chapter_title.en: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings

text: 530 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ يُسْأَلُ فِي اَلْقَبْرِ إِلاَّ مَنْ مَحَضَ اَلْإِيمَانَ مَحْضاً أَوْ مَحَضَ اَلْكُفْرَ مَحْضاً وَ اَلْبَاقُونَ مَلْهُوٌّ عَنْهُمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.530 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"No one will be questioned in the grave except one who has perfected their faith completely or one who has perfected disbelief completely. As for the rest, they will be left alone until the Day of Resurrection."
